explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「做某事」，而是指「成功完成、辦到了」。在這句話裡強調他們終於達成了某個重要目標。
generatedExample: After months of practice, they finally did it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「做某事」，而是指「成功完成、辦到了」。在這句話裡強調他們終於達成了某個重要目標。
generatedExample: After months of practice, they finally did it.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「終於、最後」。用來表示某件事經過很久之後才發生，帶有鬆一口氣或完成期待的語氣。
generatedExample: At last, the rain stopped.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `murmur` 不是「低聲說話」，而是指一種低沉、持續、幾乎聽不清的聲音，用來形容電幕被調低後發出的背景噪音。
generatedExample: The air conditioner was set to a low murmur in the background.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `richness` 不是「富有」的意思，而是指質地、色彩或感受上的濃郁與高級感。
generatedExample: The richness of the fabric made the coat look expensive.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻用法，意思是地毯非常厚、柔軟，走上去會有像踩在天鵝絨上一樣的感覺。
generatedExample: The new rug gave us the impression of treading on velvet.

explanationZhTw: `bothered to` 表示「特地去做、費心去做」。這裡是否定式，意思是他連抬頭看一下都懶得做。
generatedExample: She didn't bother to reply to the message.

explanationZhTw: `show someone in` 是慣用說法，指把某人帶進來或引進室內，不是單純「展示」。
generatedExample: The receptionist showed us in right away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指心臟劇烈跳動、怦怦直跳，通常是因為緊張、害怕或激動。
generatedExample: My heart was thumping before the interview started.

explanationZhTw: 表示「魯莽的行為」；rash 強調沒有先想清楚就做。
generatedExample: It was a rash act to quit the job without a plan.

explanationZhTw: 這是一個強調語，意思是「完全的愚蠢行為」或「十足的傻事」。
generatedExample: Trying to cross the river in a storm would be sheer folly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「走進、進入」的意思；在語境中帶有小心翼翼地進入某地的感覺。
generatedExample: We walked into the museum just before it closed.

explanationZhTw: 指「住所、居住的地方」；是比較正式、文學性的說法。
generatedExample: The old village had few permanent dwelling-places left.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「刺穿」，而是指「勉強進入、深入到某個地方」。
generatedExample: Only a few reporters penetrated into the restricted zone.

explanationZhTw: 英式英語，指「一整棟公寓大樓」。
generatedExample: They live in a modern block of flats near the station.

explanationZhTw: 指「寬敞感、空間寬闊的程度」。
generatedExample: The spaciousness of the room made it feel calm and open.

explanationZhTw: 表示「來來回回、往返地」；是固定片語。
generatedExample: People moved to and fro in the busy station hall.

explanationZhTw: 指「藉口、托詞」；表面理由不一定是真正原因。
generatedExample: She used a dentist appointment as a pretext to leave early.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「鬧鬼」，而是指「揮之不去地困擾、反覆盤旋在心頭」。
generatedExample: She was haunted by the mistake for years.

explanationZhTw: 常見片語動詞，意思是「離開、出去」，語氣可很直接。
generatedExample: Get out of the car and come inside.

explanationZhTw: 表示「毫不 اعتراض／沒有反對就接受」，demur 是較正式的「異議、反對」。
generatedExample: He signed the form without demur.

explanationZhTw: 指「毫無表情的」；臉上看不出喜怒哀樂。
generatedExample: Her expressionless face gave away nothing.

explanationZhTw: 指牆壁下半部的木板裝飾或護壁板，屬較專門的建築用語。
generatedExample: The old house had dark wood wainscoting in the hallway.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「令人害怕的、讓人感到壓迫的」。
generatedExample: The huge, silent building looked intimidating at night.

explanationZhTw: 指「骯髒的、滿是污垢的」；常用來描述灰塵、油垢或髒污。
generatedExample: The windows were grimy after months without cleaning.

explanationZhTw: 一小張紙；這裡的 slip 指薄片、小張紙條。
generatedExample: He handed me a slip of paper with the address written on it.

explanationZhTw: 專注地、聚精會神地。
generatedExample: She listened intently to every word of the interview.

explanationZhTw: 令人敬畏的；在這裡偏向『有威嚴、讓人畏懼』，不是單純的『強大』。
generatedExample: The judge gave him a formidable look before speaking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stirring 不是『攪動』，而是『動一下、移動』；整句就是『一動不動』。
generatedExample: The cat stayed on the windowsill without stirring.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的裝置名稱，指把說話內容直接記錄成文字的機器。
generatedExample: She dictated the note into the speakwrite and sent it off.

explanationZhTw: 迅速而生硬地說出；常帶有不耐煩、命令式的語氣。
generatedExample: The sergeant rapped out the orders and marched away.

explanationZhTw: 混雜的行話／專門用語；hybrid 表示混合的，jargon 表示特定群體才懂的語言。
generatedExample: The manual was written in a hybrid jargon of science and marketing.

explanationZhTw: `fullwise` 是 Newspeak 風格的副詞，意思接近「完全地、徹底地」。在這裡是刻意造出的用法，不是一般標準英文。
generatedExample: The plan was approved fullwise after the final review.

explanationZhTw: `doubleplus` 是《1984》裡的 Newspeak 強化語，相當於「非常、極度」。`doubleplus ridiculous` 就是「極其荒謬」。
generatedExample: The proposal sounded doubleplus ridiculous to everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: `verging on` 表示「快要接近某種狀態」。這裡的 `verging` 是在說前面的內容接近某個極端判斷。
generatedExample: His comment was verging on an insult.

explanationZhTw: `crimethink` 是《1984》的專有 Newspeak 詞，指「思想犯罪、違反政權允許的思想」。
generatedExample: In the novel, even silent disagreement could be labeled crimethink.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `cancel` 不是日常最常見的「取消行程」，而更像電報式的「撤銷、作廢、否決」用法。
generatedExample: The committee decided to cancel the previous instruction.

explanationZhTw: `unproceed` 是刻意造詞，表示「不繼續進行、停止前進」。它不是標準英文詞彙，而是仿 Newspeak 的反向構詞。
generatedExample: If the numbers do not add up, unproceed with the launch.

explanationZhTw: `-wise` 在這裡表示「就……而言、在……方面」。`constructionwise` 就是「就建造/構造方面而言」。
generatedExample: Constructionwise, the new bridge is much stronger than the old one.

explanationZhTw: `antegetting` 是仿拉丁前綴 `ante-` 的造詞，意思近似「在取得之前、預先取得」。屬於刻意扭曲的政治語言。
generatedExample: We should avoid antegetting permission before the details are ready.

explanationZhTw: `plusfull` 是強化式造詞，語感上接近「完全加上、充分增加」。不是標準英文，而是新聞話語式的拼接詞。
generatedExample: The team asked for plusfull funding before starting the project.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 atmosphere 不是「大氣」，而是「氛圍、氣氛」；official atmosphere 指官方/體制感很強的氛圍。
generatedExample: The office had an official atmosphere that made everyone speak carefully.

explanationZhTw: Newspeak 是《一九八四》中的「新語」，一種被政權操控、用來限制思想的官方語言。
generatedExample: She learned several Newspeak words to understand the novel's politics.

explanationZhTw: 指「政治陰謀者／策劃顛覆的人」，通常暗示秘密反抗政府或政權。
generatedExample: The novel follows a political conspirator who works in secret.

explanationZhTw: equivocal 表示「模稜兩可的、不明確的」；這裡指那句話含糊到難以確定真正意思。
generatedExample: Her equivocal remark left everyone unsure of her real opinion.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「退而求其次依靠／改用」某個方法、理由或藉口。
generatedExample: When the plan failed, we had to fall back on an old strategy.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「藉口、假裝」；指為掩飾真實目的而編出的說法。
generatedExample: Her smile was only a pretence to hide how angry she felt.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的專有詞，指兼具螢幕與監控功能的裝置；在小說裡是政府監視人民的工具。
generatedExample: Everyone stayed silent because the telescreen could hear them.

explanationZhTw: 「uttered」在這裡是「發出／說出」的意思，比一般的 say 更正式，常用來描述聲音或話語被說出口。
generatedExample: She uttered a brief apology and walked away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「squeak」不是一般指老鼠叫聲，而是指「因驚訝而發出的細小尖短聲」。
generatedExample: He let out a squeak of surprise when the lights suddenly came on.

explanationZhTw: 「in the midst of」表示「在……之中；正處於……期間」，帶有較書面的語氣。
generatedExample: In the midst of the storm, the town lost power.

explanationZhTw: 「taken aback」是慣用語，表示「愣住；大吃一驚；被嚇到一時說不出話」。
generatedExample: She was taken aback by the sudden question.

explanationZhTw: 「hold his tongue」表示「忍住不說；保持沉默」，常用來指不把想法直接講出口。
generatedExample: He wanted to complain, but he held his tongue.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「把它關掉／停止運作」。這裡是字面用法，指把某個裝置或機器關閉。
generatedExample: If the fan is too noisy, you can turn it off at night.

explanationZhTw: 「turn it off」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「把它關掉／關閉」。
generatedExample: Please turn it off before you leave the room.

explanationZhTw: 「privilege」在這裡不是一般「特權」的抽象意思，而是指「被允許做某事的權限／資格」。
generatedExample: Only staff have the privilege of entering this area.

explanationZhTw: 高高壓過；在高度或氣勢上明顯超過。這裡強調他站姿帶來的壓迫感。
generatedExample: The old castle towered over the small village below.

explanationZhTw: 無法辨認的；看不透的。這裡指他臉上的表情難以讀出意思。
generatedExample: Her expression was indecipherable, so I could not tell what she thought.

explanationZhTw: 可以想像的；有可能的。這裡表示那個推測仍然說得通。
generatedExample: It is conceivable that the train will be late because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 打斷；使中斷。這裡是說他被人打擾、話被截斷了。
generatedExample: Sorry for interrupting you, but I need to ask a quick question.

explanationZhTw: 電視螢幕式監視器；《一九八四》裡的特定裝置名稱。不是一般日常用語。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never stopped broadcasting propaganda.

explanationZhTw: 死寂的；非常安靜到可怕。這裡不是字面上的「致命」，而是強調極度安靜。
generatedExample: When the music stopped, the hall became deadly silent.

explanationZhTw: （時間）一分一秒地流逝；帶有比喻意味。這裡把秒數寫成像隊伍一樣前進。
generatedExample: The hours marched past while we waited for the news.

explanationZhTw: （表情）鬆動、瓦解；轉變成另一種樣子。這裡不是「故障」，而是臉上的嚴肅表情開始融化。
generatedExample: Her stern expression broke down into a warm smile.

explanationZhTw: 「say」在這裡是最基本的「說、講出來」；指把話說出口，問的是誰來把那句話說出來。
generatedExample: Please say your name clearly when they call you.

explanationZhTw: 「promptly」在這裡表示「立刻、迅速地、毫不拖延地」。不是「有禮貌地」的意思，而是說溫斯頓回答得很快。
generatedExample: She promptly answered the question.

explanationZhTw: 「turn off」是片語動詞，這裡指「把……關掉、停止運作」。句中是在確認那個東西真的已經被關閉了。
generatedExample: Please turn off the lights before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「關掉／切斷電源；使停止運作」。這裡指所有裝置或系統都已經關閉。
generatedExample: Please make sure the lights are turned off before you leave.

explanationZhTw: `because` 是表示原因的連接詞；在這裡後面接破折號，表示說話被打斷，原因沒有說完。
generatedExample: She left early because she felt ill.

explanationZhTw: 停頓、暫停一下；這裡表示他先停止動作或說話，稍微思考。
generatedExample: She paused before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 模糊、不明確；這裡指他的動機其實並不清楚。
generatedExample: There was a vagueness in his plan that worried everyone.

explanationZhTw: 其實、實際上；用來修正或強調前面的說法，表示真相和表面不同。
generatedExample: I thought he agreed, but in fact he had some doubts.

explanationZhTw: 期待、預期；這裡指他原本想從奧布萊恩那裡得到某種幫助。
generatedExample: We expected a quick reply from the company.

explanationZhTw: 無力的、薄弱的；這裡指他的話聽起來沒有說服力，也不夠有力。
generatedExample: His feeble apology made the situation worse.

explanationZhTw: 裝腔作勢的、矯飾的；這裡指他的話聽起來刻意賣弄、想顯得比實際更高明。
generatedExample: The review called the film pretentious and hard to enjoy.

explanationZhTw: 指「陰謀」或「串謀」，比一般的計畫更帶有秘密、非法、敵對的意味。
generatedExample: They suspected a conspiracy to take over the company.

explanationZhTw: 指「秘密組織」，這裡暗示的是地下、隱蔽的反抗團體。
generatedExample: The police searched for a secret organization operating in the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「牽涉其中、參與其中」的意思，不是單純的「涉及」。
generatedExample: Her name was not on the list, so she was not involved in it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「為它工作」，而是「替它效力、為它做事」。
generatedExample: He said he would work for the cause until it succeeded.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》裡，`Party` 不是「派對」，而是「政黨」，特指統治政權。
generatedExample: Everyone in the novel feared the Party.

explanationZhTw: 指「原則、信條」，這裡是某個政治體系的核心信念。
generatedExample: She refused to compromise her principles.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的造詞，指「思想犯」；連想法本身都被政權視為犯罪。
generatedExample: In the story, even private doubts could mark someone as a thought-criminal.

explanationZhTw: 指「通姦者」；這是較正式、法律或道德語境中的詞。
generatedExample: The novel treated adulterers as enemies of the state.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「把自己完全交由你處置／任你發落」。
generatedExample: After the mistake, we had no choice but to put ourselves at their mercy.

explanationZhTw: 法律語境用法，指「使自己受牽連而入罪」或「自我供罪」。
generatedExample: He refused to incriminate himself during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「回頭瞄一眼／往肩後看」，表示他警覺地確認身後發生了什麼事，不是單純隨便看一眼。
generatedExample: She glanced over her shoulder before crossing the street.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「果然／不出所料」，常用來表示前面的猜測被證實了。
generatedExample: Sure enough, the train was delayed by twenty minutes.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「進來／走進來」。
generatedExample: Please come in and have a seat.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「醒酒瓶／倒酒瓶」，通常是用來盛酒、倒酒的玻璃容器。
generatedExample: The waiter brought a decanter of water to the table.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「我們這邊的人、自己人」。
generatedExample: After a week with the team, he felt like one of us.

explanationZhTw: 「面無表情地、冷淡地」；表示說話時沒有流露情緒。
generatedExample: She answered impassively, so I could not tell what she was thinking.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「不如、倒不如」；常用來說某個選擇和別的選擇差不多。
generatedExample: If we are already here, we may as well finish the work now.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「生意」，而是「正事、嚴肅的事務」；屬於語境上的特殊用法。
generatedExample: Let's stop joking, because this is business.

explanationZhTw: 「at his ease」表示「很自在、很放鬆」；這裡描寫他坐得很從容，不緊張。
generatedExample: She sat at her ease and listened to the conversation.

explanationZhTw: 「valet」指「男僕、侍者」；這裡用來形容他帶有一種僕役般的氣質。
generatedExample: The valet carried the guest's coat upstairs.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「用眼角餘光看、斜眼偷看」。
generatedExample: He checked the hallway out of the corner of his eye.

explanationZhTw: 「playing a part」在這裡不是字面上的「演出」，而是指「假裝成某種角色、偽裝自己」。
generatedExample: At school he was always playing a part to fit in.

explanationZhTw: 「decanter」是「醒酒器、玻璃酒壺」的意思。
generatedExample: She poured the wine from the decanter into two glasses.

explanationZhTw: 「filled up」是片語動詞，意思是「裝滿、倒滿」。
generatedExample: He filled up the bowl with water.

explanationZhTw: 「hoarding」在英式英文裡可指「廣告看板、大型招貼板」；這裡是較特殊的用法。
generatedExample: The slogan was painted on a hoarding near the station.

explanationZhTw: 「get to」這裡是「送達、到達」的意思，表示酒很少能送到外黨。
generatedExample: Hardly any fresh fruit gets to our town in winter.

explanationZhTw: 「solemn」是「莊嚴的、凝重的、嚴肅的」，形容他的表情變得正式而沉重。
generatedExample: The judge spoke in a solemn voice.

explanationZhTw: 「raise his glass」在這裡是「舉起酒杯」並準備敬酒的意思。
generatedExample: She raised her glass and thanked everyone for coming.

explanationZhTw: 「fitting」這裡是「恰當的、適合的」；語氣帶有正式和儀式感。
generatedExample: It felt fitting to start the ceremony with a quiet song.

explanationZhTw: 這是較老式的說法，意思是「為某人乾杯、祝某人健康」。
generatedExample: At dinner, they drank a health to the retiring teacher.

explanationZhTw: `take up` 這裡是「拿起、舉起」的意思，屬於常見片語動詞，不是「開始從事」的用法。
generatedExample: She took up the glass and looked through it.

explanationZhTw: `eagerness` 指「急切、渴望」，表示他很想立刻去做某件事。
generatedExample: He opened the envelope with eagerness.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `romantic` 不是「浪漫愛情」的意思，而是「理想化的、帶懷舊色彩的」。
generatedExample: She had a romantic idea of life in the countryside.

explanationZhTw: `olden time` 是較文學、老派的說法，意思是「很久以前、古早年代」。
generatedExample: In olden time, people traveled by horse.

explanationZhTw: `swallow` 這裡是「吞下、喝下」；屬於常見動詞。
generatedExample: He swallowed the medicine with water.

explanationZhTw: `barely` 表示「幾乎不、勉強才」，強調程度非常低。
generatedExample: I barely knew anyone at the party.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有這樣一個人／有名叫……的人」，用來強調對某個人的存在表示懷疑或確認。
generatedExample: I didn't know there was such a person as Dr. Vale.

explanationZhTw: `alive` 表示「活著的；尚未死亡的」。這裡是在確認某人仍然生存，屬於基本但很常用的 A2 字。
generatedExample: After the accident, everyone was relieved that she was alive.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指秘密的陰謀或共謀。在這裡是指一個隱密組織或計畫。
generatedExample: She suspected a conspiracy to hide the truth from the public.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是「發明」的意思，而是「捏造的東西、憑空編造的說法」。
generatedExample: The rumor was not an invention; it was based on real events.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指負責監控思想、壓制異議的秘密警察機構。
generatedExample: Everyone feared the Thought Police would discover their private doubts.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指一個秘密組織或地下團體，不是一般的「兄弟會」字面意思。
generatedExample: She was told the Brotherhood operated in secret across the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「稍後、待會兒」，不是現代常見的「目前」。
generatedExample: I will explain the plan presently, after I finish this part.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是專有名詞，指體制中最核心、權力最高的階層。
generatedExample: Only members of the Inner Party were allowed to enter the building.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語動詞意思是「關掉、停止運作」。
generatedExample: Please turn off the lights before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的虛構名詞，指兼具螢幕與監視功能的裝置。
generatedExample: The telescreen watched everyone in the room.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，意思是「可供我們使用、由我們支配」。
generatedExample: We only have one hour at our disposal, so we must work quickly.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有能力做某事；能夠……」，常接 `of + 名詞/動名詞`。
generatedExample: She is capable of handling difficult situations calmly.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「理所當然地認為；不加思索地假設」。這裡是說 O'Brien 直接假定溫斯頓可以代表茱莉亞發言。
generatedExample: She took it for granted that I would help, but she never asked me.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，表示「沒有表情的；面無表情的」。這裡形容他的聲音冷淡、沒有情緒起伏。
generatedExample: Her expressionless face made it hard to tell what she was thinking.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，這裡不是一般的「例行公事」而是指「像例行流程一樣的固定問答」。
generatedExample: For the doctor, the checkup was just routine.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，原本指宗教教理問答；這裡是比喻用法，表示「一套固定的問答流程」。
generatedExample: The interviewer followed a strict catechism of questions.

explanationZhTw: 「prepared」表示「願意、已做好心理準備」，在這裡是指一個人是否已經下定決心去做危險的事。
generatedExample: Are you prepared to speak in public tomorrow?

explanationZhTw: 「give your lives」在這裡不是字面上的「把生命交出去」，而是指「犧牲生命」；屬於語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The soldiers were willing to give their lives to protect the village.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `prepared` 不是單純指「已準備好東西」，而是指「你有心理準備、願意去做某事」；在這句話裡帶有質疑對方是否真的下定決心的意味。
generatedExample: Are you really prepared to leave everything behind for this job?

explanationZhTw: `commit murder` 是正式、法律或敘述中常見的搭配，意思是「犯下謀殺罪」。這裡不是日常口語的隨便說法，而是帶有嚴肅指控意味的表達。
generatedExample: If the evidence is strong enough, the court will decide whether he committed murder.

explanationZhTw: 「進行破壞行為」；`sabotage` 指故意破壞、使計畫或設備失效。在這裡是正式、帶有政治或軍事意味的用法。
generatedExample: They were accused of planning to commit acts of sabotage against the railway.

explanationZhTw: 這是正式說法，意思是「導致……死亡」；比 `kill` 更書面，也較常見於新聞、法律或嚴肅敘述。
generatedExample: The collapse of the bridge could cause the death of many workers.

explanationZhTw: `innocent` 指「無辜的、未犯錯的」。`innocent people` 強調這些人不該受到傷害。
generatedExample: The explosion injured innocent people who were walking nearby.

explanationZhTw: 「betray your country」指「背叛自己的國家／出賣祖國」，是帶有強烈政治與道德色彩的用法，不只是一般的「欺騙」。
generatedExample: He was accused of betraying his country during the war.

explanationZhTw: 「foreign powers」指「外國勢力／外國政府」，在政治語境中常用來泛指其他國家的權力或政府。
generatedExample: The treaty was designed to stop foreign powers from вмешing in local affairs.

explanationZhTw: 作弊；在這裡指為了達成目的而違規或以不誠實手段行事。
generatedExample: They cheated on the exam and were both suspended.

explanationZhTw: 偽造；在這裡不是「鍛造」，而是指製作假的文件、簽名或證據。
generatedExample: Someone forged her signature on the contract.

explanationZhTw: 勒索；用威脅公開秘密或造成傷害來逼迫對方做事。
generatedExample: He tried to blackmail the manager for extra money.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「敗壞、腐蝕」思想或品格，不是金錢上的貪腐。
generatedExample: The group accused the media of trying to corrupt young people.

explanationZhTw: 分發；把東西送到多個人或多個地方。
generatedExample: Volunteers distributed food to the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 會讓人上癮的；常用來形容某種物質或行為容易形成依賴。
generatedExample: This medicine is effective but habit-forming if misused.

explanationZhTw: 鼓勵；促使某事更可能發生或讓人更願意去做。
generatedExample: Parents should encourage children to ask questions.

explanationZhTw: 賣淫；以性行為換取金錢或其他利益。
generatedExample: The report examined how poverty can push people into prostitution.

explanationZhTw: 散播、傳播；通常用於資訊、思想、消息或疾病的擴散。
generatedExample: False rumors can disseminate quickly on social media.

explanationZhTw: 性病；透過性接觸傳播的疾病。
generatedExample: The clinic offers testing and treatment for venereal diseases.

explanationZhTw: 士氣低落、道德敗壞；在這裡指讓人失去信心或道德感的狀態。
generatedExample: The long defeat caused demoralization in the team.

explanationZhTw: 削弱；使力量、影響力或效果變小。
generatedExample: Lack of sleep can weaken your concentration.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `serve our interests` 不是單純「服務我們的利益」的字面意思，而是指「對我們有利、符合我們的目的」；在這種語境下是比較正式、帶策略意味的用法。
generatedExample: The new policy serves our interests by lowering operating costs.

explanationZhTw: `prepared to` 在這裡不是「準備好物品」的意思，而是指「願意、打算承受某件事」。整句是在質問對方是否願意接受這種人生轉變。
generatedExample: Are you prepared to move to another city for the job?

explanationZhTw: `lose your identity` 在這裡是比喻說法，意思是「失去自我、原本的身分感或個人認同」，不是字面上的弄丟證件。
generatedExample: He felt he would lose his identity if he changed his name and left his hometown.

explanationZhTw: `live out` 是片語動詞，意思是「把餘生過完／度過」，常和 `the rest of your life` 連用。
generatedExample: She decided to live out her remaining years near the sea.

explanationZhTw: `dock-worker` 指「碼頭工人」，是在港口或碼頭搬運、裝卸貨物的人。
generatedExample: My grandfather worked as a dock-worker when he was young.

explanationZhTw: 「準備好／願意」；這裡表示你已經心理上接受並能照做。
generatedExample: I am prepared to leave early if needed.

explanationZhTw: 「自殺」；較正式的說法，指自己結束生命。
generatedExample: The doctor asked whether the patient had thoughts of committing suicide.

explanationZhTw: 「如果以及在……的時候」；很正式，常表示事情可能發生，但時間不確定。
generatedExample: If and when you arrive, send me a message.

explanationZhTw: 「這麼做／做那件事」；用來代替前面已提過的動作，這裡指代前面的「自殺」。
generatedExample: She may call later, and if she does so, please answer politely.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「有心理準備、願意接受」；不是單純「已經準備好東西」，而是指是否接受接下來的結果。
generatedExample: Are you prepared to move to another city if the job requires it?

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動詞，意思是「分開、拆散」，特別指兩個人不再在一起。
generatedExample: They decided to separate after many years together.

explanationZhTw: 表示「彼此、互相」，這裡指兩個人之間不再見面。
generatedExample: The two friends helped one another during the move.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「突然插話、打斷別人說話」，不是字面上的「闖入」。
generatedExample: He broke in before I could finish my sentence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `appeared to` 不是「出現」，而是「看起來／似乎」；表示 Winston 的主觀感受。
generatedExample: It appeared to me that the meeting would never end.

explanationZhTw: `deprived of` 表示「被剝奪／失去」某種能力或權利，這裡是說他一時說不出話。
generatedExample: The storm deprived the town of electricity for two days.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，指「說話能力」或「發言的能力」。
generatedExample: She was so shocked that she lost the power of speech.

explanationZhTw: `soundlessly` 表示「沒有聲音地、無聲地」，用來描述動作完全安靜。
generatedExample: He opened the door soundlessly so he would not wake anyone.

explanationZhTw: `opening syllables` 在這裡不是一般「開頭的音節」的教科書用法，而是指話語剛開始冒出來的聲音片段。
generatedExample: She could only manage the opening syllables of his name.

explanationZhTw: 這是常見片語，表示「一再地、反覆地」。
generatedExample: I checked the message over and over again to make sure it was correct.

explanationZhTw: `finally` 表示「終於、最後」，常用來表示等了很久之後才發生。
generatedExample: After several tries, she finally opened the jar.

explanationZhTw: 「did well」在這裡不是單純指「表現好」，而是表示「做得對／做得明智」。整句是在稱讚對方把事情告訴他是正確的。
generatedExample: You did well to ask for help before making a decision.

explanationZhTw: 「necessary」表示「有必要的、必須的」。這裡強調「我們必須知道所有事情」，語氣很直接。
generatedExample: It is necessary to arrive early for the interview.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `added` 不是「增加」，而是指「接著說、補充說」。在敘事中常用來表示同一個人繼續開口說話。
generatedExample: He paused, then added that he would call later.

explanationZhTw: `expression` 在這裡不是「表情」的字面意思，而是指聲音裡帶有比較多的情感、語氣變化。
generatedExample: Her voice had more expression when she talked about her family.

explanationZhTw: 「survive」表示「活下來、倖存」。這裡是指那個人即使沒死，也可能以另一種狀態活著。
generatedExample: He may survive the accident, but he will need a long recovery.

explanationZhTw: 「be obliged to」表示「不得不、必須」，語氣比 `have to` 更正式，也帶有被情勢迫使的感覺。
generatedExample: We were obliged to cancel the trip because of the storm.

explanationZhTw: 「identity」在這裡是「身分、身份」。不是指抽象的自我認同，而是可被重新安排的個人身分資料。
generatedExample: After the move, she decided to keep her identity private.

explanationZhTw: 「movements」這裡是「動作、肢體動作」。不是泛指移動，而是人身體表現出的細微動作。
generatedExample: Her movements were slow because she was tired.

explanationZhTw: 「colour」是「顏色」，這裡特別指頭髮的顏色。這也是英式拼法，美式通常寫作 `color`。
generatedExample: The colour of the walls made the room feel brighter.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「把人改變到完全認不出來」。`beyond recognition` 是固定用法，強調變化大到無法辨認。
generatedExample: The fire altered the building beyond recognition.

explanationZhTw: 「amputate」是醫療用語，表示「截除、截肢」，通常指把受傷或壞死的肢體部分切除。
generatedExample: Doctors had to amputate the leg after the severe infection.

explanationZhTw: 「limb」指「肢體」或「四肢之一」，例如手臂或腿。
generatedExample: He lost a limb in the accident and learned to use a prosthetic leg.

explanationZhTw: 「could not help + V-ing」表示「忍不住、無法不……」，是很常見的慣用法。這裡是說溫斯頓忍不住又偷看了一眼。
generatedExample: I could not help laughing when I heard the joke.

explanationZhTw: 「sidelong glance」是「斜眼一瞥、側目一看」。帶有不直接、偷偷觀察的語氣。
generatedExample: She gave him a sidelong glance before answering.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「Mongolian」不是單純指國籍，而是舊式、帶民族學色彩的外貌描述，用來形容臉部特徵。閱讀時要按語境理解，不要直譯成現代地理意義。
generatedExample: The old book used an outdated term to describe the man's face.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「face」是動詞，意思是「直面、勇敢對著某人／某事」。不是名詞「臉」。
generatedExample: He faced the judge without looking away.

explanationZhTw: 「murmured」表示「低聲說、喃喃說」，聲音很小、不清楚。
generatedExample: She murmured an apology and left the room.

explanationZhTw: 「assent」是正式用語，意思是「同意、贊同」。這裡表示她低聲說了些像是在表示同意的話。
generatedExample: The committee gave its assent to the proposal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `settled` 不是單純指「安置」或「沉澱」，而是表示「事情已經決定好了、定案了」。屬於常見但容易依上下文誤讀的用法。
generatedExample: Once we agreed on the budget, the plan was settled.

explanationZhTw: adj. 心不在焉的、漫不經心的。這裡形容 O'Brien 的神情，表示他不是專注地在做事，而是有點分神、若有所思。
generatedExample: She gave me an absent-minded smile while reading the notes.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「來回、反覆地」。這裡指 O'Brien 在房間裡慢慢來回踱步。
generatedExample: The dog ran to and fro across the yard.

explanationZhTw: n. 絲滑感、絲般的柔順觸感。這裡是形容香菸紙的觸感很細緻，屬於較不常見的感官用法。
generatedExample: The fabric had a silkiness that made it feel expensive.

explanationZhTw: `had better` 表示「最好……／應該……」，帶有勸告或警告的語氣，比單純的建議更強。
generatedExample: You had better leave now if you want to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: `switch on` 是片語動詞，意思是「打開電源／開啟」。在這裡是指稍後把某個裝置或系統打開。
generatedExample: Please switch on the lamp before you start reading.

explanationZhTw: `take a good look` 是慣用說法，意思是「仔細看看」。通常暗示要記清楚、看得很認真。
generatedExample: Take a good look at the map before we leave.

explanationZhTw: `see` 在這裡不是單純「用眼睛看見」，而是指「再遇到／再見到」；屬於依上下文才明確的意思。
generatedExample: I hope to see you again next month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「閃爍」，而是指眼神快速掃過他們的臉，帶有冷淡、戒備的感覺。
generatedExample: Her eyes flickered over the crowd before she answered.

explanationZhTw: 固定說法，表示「一點痕跡／跡象也沒有」。
generatedExample: There was not a trace of fear in his voice.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「人造的、非天然的」，用來強調這張臉不自然。
generatedExample: The jacket was made from synthetic fabric.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無法做某事、沒有能力做某事」，語氣比一般的 can’t 更正式。
generatedExample: She was incapable of hiding her disappointment.

explanationZhTw: 正式用語，指「問候、致意」。
generatedExample: The letter began with a formal salutation.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡表示「走出去、離開房間」。
generatedExample: He went out after dinner and came back late.

explanationZhTw: 指「工作服、連身工作褲」，常見於勞動或工作場合。
generatedExample: The mechanic wiped his hands on his overalls.

explanationZhTw: 不知道內情；對情況一無所知。
generatedExample: We were in the dark about the plan until the last minute.

explanationZhTw: 服從他們；照著指示做。
generatedExample: The soldiers had to obey them immediately.

explanationZhTw: 正式成員；完全被接納的成員。
generatedExample: After the training, we became full members of the team.

explanationZhTw: 總體目標；整體想達成的事情。
generatedExample: The general aims of the project are clear to everyone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 numbers 是「有……人數／總共有……」的意思。
generatedExample: The club numbers about fifty members.

explanationZhTw: 多達十二個；強調數量上限。
generatedExample: Only as many as a dozen people came to the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 與你聯絡；和你溝通。
generatedExample: Please communicate with me by email if anything changes.

explanationZhTw: a handful of 表示「少數幾個」；不是字面上的一把。
generatedExample: Only a handful of unimportant people knew the secret.

explanationZhTw: 來回地、前後往返地走動。
generatedExample: She paced to and fro while waiting for the call.

explanationZhTw: 笨重、臃腫的狀態；這裡指身形雖大卻顯得笨重。
generatedExample: The bulkiness of the package made it hard to carry upstairs.

explanationZhTw: 猛地伸入、快速用力地插入；此處是很突然地把手伸進口袋。
generatedExample: He thrust his hands into his coat and walked outside.

explanationZhTw: 熟練地擺弄、靈巧地操作；這裡指很自然地轉弄香菸。
generatedExample: She manipulated the controls until the screen focused clearly.

explanationZhTw: 帶有一點、略微染上某種色彩；這裡是「帶著些許諷刺意味」。
generatedExample: His reply was tinged with sadness.

explanationZhTw: 一心一意、專注到底的特質。
generatedExample: Her single-mindedness helped her finish the project on time.

explanationZhTw: 輕佻的調侃、近乎戲謔的說話方式；語氣故意不嚴肅。
generatedExample: He covered his nervousness with persiflage during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 毫不退縮地、堅定不移地。
generatedExample: She faced the criticism unflinchingly.

explanationZhTw: 模糊的、影影綽綽的；也可指不明確、難以捉摸。
generatedExample: A shadowy figure stood near the gate.

explanationZhTw: 計謀、策略；通常指為達目的而設計的巧妙手段。
generatedExample: The general used a stratagem to draw the enemy out.

explanationZhTw: 有能力應付、勝任；這裡是「沒有他應付不了的計謀」。
generatedExample: She was equal to the task and finished it quickly.

explanationZhTw: 「rumours」是指未經證實的傳聞、謠言。
generatedExample: I heard rumours that the shop will close next month.

explanationZhTw: 「the existence of」表示「……的存在」。
generatedExample: They discussed the existence of life on Mars.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，表示先在腦中形成自己對某事的想像或印象。
generatedExample: Before the interview, I had formed my own picture of the company.

explanationZhTw: 「underworld」在這裡不是單純的「地下世界」，而是指秘密活動、陰暗隱密的圈子。
generatedExample: The novel describes an underworld of spies and smugglers.

explanationZhTw: 「conspirators」是指密謀者、共謀者。
generatedExample: The police arrested the conspirators after the attack.

explanationZhTw: 「cellars」是地下室或地窖。
generatedExample: They hid the boxes in the cellar.

explanationZhTw: 「codewords」是暗號、密碼詞。
generatedExample: The team used codewords to identify each other.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用語，表示「落入……手中」，通常帶有被控制、被抓住的意思。
generatedExample: The secret document fell into the hands of the wrong person.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「lead ... to ...」表示「使……查到／導向……」。
generatedExample: The clue led them to the hidden room.

explanationZhTw: 「wiped out」是片語動詞，表示徹底消滅、根除。
generatedExample: The virus was wiped out in the region.

explanationZhTw: 「holds it together」表示讓某個團體或系統維持在一起；這裡指維繫組織的力量。
generatedExample: Shared values hold the team together.

explanationZhTw: 「At most」表示「頂多、至多」。
generatedExample: At most, the trip will take two hours.

explanationZhTw: 「smuggle」是走私、偷偷運送或夾帶。
generatedExample: They tried to smuggle phones into the prison.

explanationZhTw: 「get used to」表示「逐漸習慣」。
generatedExample: You will get used to the new schedule soon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「without results」是指「沒有成果、沒有回報」。
generatedExample: We worked for hours without results.

explanationZhTw: 「perceptible」表示「可察覺的、看得出來的」。
generatedExample: There was a perceptible shift in his tone.

explanationZhTw: 「take part in」表示「參與、加入」。
generatedExample: I will take part in the meeting tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 這是具象比喻，指人死後只剩下極少的殘骸，強調渺小與悲涼。
generatedExample: The poem speaks of us as handfuls of dust and bone.

explanationZhTw: 「At present」表示「目前、現在」。
generatedExample: At present, we are waiting for the results.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻說法，表示慢慢擴大理性與正常狀態能存在的範圍。
generatedExample: We can only rebuild trust little by little.

explanationZhTw: 「act collectively」表示一起行動、集體行動。
generatedExample: The workers decided to act collectively.

explanationZhTw: 「in the face of」是慣用語，表示「面對、在……壓力下」。
generatedExample: In the face of strong opposition, she continued.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「halted」是「停下來、停住」的意思，比一般的 `stop` 更正式；在這個句子中指他突然停步。
generatedExample: She halted at the door when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 「wrist-watch」是「腕錶；手錶」的意思，指戴在手腕上的錶。
generatedExample: He checked his wrist-watch before boarding the train.

explanationZhTw: 「comrade」在這裡不是一般「朋友」，而是帶有政治/意識形態色彩的稱呼，常見於共產主義或集體主義語境中，意思接近「同志」。
generatedExample: The officer nodded and said, 'Comrade, please report to the meeting room.'

explanationZhTw: 「decanter」是指倒酒用的醒酒器或酒壺，屬於較具體的器具詞彙，日常口語不算常見。
generatedExample: She poured the wine from the decanter into two glasses.

explanationZhTw: 「half full」在這裡是字面意思，表示還有一半液體，重點是容器沒有空掉。
generatedExample: The bottle is only half full, so we need another one.

explanationZhTw: `fill` 在這裡是「把……裝滿、倒滿」的意思。
generatedExample: She filled the bottle with water.

explanationZhTw: `raise` 在這裡是「舉起、抬起」的意思。
generatedExample: He raised his hand to speak.

explanationZhTw: `stem` 在這裡不是植物的「莖」，而是酒杯、香檳杯或紅酒杯底部連接杯身與杯腳的細長部位；`by the stem` 就是「拿著杯梗」的意思。
generatedExample: She held the wine glass by the stem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「困惑」，而是表示「讓……難堪、出糗、蒙羞」。整個語氣帶有諷刺和挑釁意味。
generatedExample: The project succeeded, to the confusion of everyone who had doubted it.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡的專有名詞，指監控人民思想、打壓異議的秘密警察或思想審查機構。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Thought Police watch for even the slightest sign of rebellion.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》裡的專有名詞，指全知全控的極權領袖或象徵性的監視權力。
generatedExample: People used the slogan to protest against Big Brother and constant surveillance.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `past` 指「過去、往昔」；整個片語是在表示把焦點放回過去，不是一般的方向用法。
generatedExample: She often looked to the past for clues about how her family had changed.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是名詞，指「過去、往昔」；常用來談經歷、歷史或回憶。
generatedExample: She still thinks about the past from time to time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「嚴重地」，而是表示說話態度「嚴肅地、鄭重地」。
generatedExample: He answered gravely, as if the decision could not be changed.

explanationZhTw: 「stood up」在這裡是「站起身來」；指從坐著變成站立，是很常見的動作片語。
generatedExample: When the train arrived, everyone stood up to get off.

explanationZhTw: 「go out」這裡是「出去／離開（室內或某處）」；不是「熄滅」或其他意思。
generatedExample: We should go out early to avoid the traffic.

explanationZhTw: 「lift」是英式英語的「電梯」；在這裡不是「抬起」，而是指建築物裡的升降設備。
generatedExample: Take the lift to the sixth floor.

explanationZhTw: 「observant」是「善於觀察、很留意細節」；這裡表示電梯服務員很可能注意到異狀。
generatedExample: The observant teacher noticed that one student was missing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「忘記她存在」；而是指「彷彿當她不存在、故意不理會她」。
generatedExample: After the argument, he acted as if my existence no longer mattered.

explanationZhTw: 「pace」在這裡是名詞，意思是「一步、一步的距離」；「a pace or two」就是「一兩步」。
generatedExample: She moved a pace or two closer to the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `settled` 不是「定居」，而是「敲定、安排妥當、決定好」。
generatedExample: We still need to settle the venue and the schedule.

explanationZhTw: `hiding-place` 指「藏身處、藏匿地點」。
generatedExample: The old cellar could serve as a hiding-place if we needed one.

explanationZhTw: `explain` 表示「說明、解釋」，這裡是指溫斯頓把那個房間的情況講清楚。
generatedExample: She explained the plan to me in simple words.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `over` 不是「超過」的意思，而是表示「在……上方、樓上」。
generatedExample: They live in the apartment over the bakery.

explanationZhTw: `shop` 指「商店、店鋪」。在英式英語裡很常見，泛指賣東西的小店。
generatedExample: I bought bread at the shop near my house.

explanationZhTw: 「for the moment」表示「暫時、目前先這樣」，暗示只是暫時安排，不是永久決定。
generatedExample: For the moment, we should wait and see.

explanationZhTw: 「get hold of」在這裡是「設法拿到、弄到手」；常用來表示取得某物或聯絡到某人。
generatedExample: I tried to get hold of her after class.

explanationZhTw: 「in existence」指「現存的、仍存在的」；這裡強調這種東西數量很少，但還沒消失。
generatedExample: Only a few copies are still in existence.

explanationZhTw: 「hunt them down」是「追捕、搜捕直到找到」；比單純的「找」更強烈，帶有追查和抓到的意思。
generatedExample: The police hunted the thief down within hours.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「差別很小、幾乎沒有影響」；句子是在說結果不太重要。
generatedExample: Whether we leave now or later makes very little difference.

explanationZhTw: 「indestructible」是「不可摧毀的、無法被破壞的」。
generatedExample: They made the case out of an indestructible material.

explanationZhTw: 「word for word」是固定說法，意思是「逐字逐句、完全照原文」；通常指一個字都不改地重複。
generatedExample: Please repeat the instructions word for word.

explanationZhTw: 「brief-case」是「公事包、文件包」，通常用來裝工作文件。
generatedExample: He put the contract in his brief-case.

explanationZhTw: 「as a rule」表示「通常、一般而言、按慣例」，用來說明大多數情況下的做法或結果。
generatedExample: As a rule, we meet on Fridays, but this week is an exception.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「like」不是「喜歡」或「像」，而是用在「What is it like?」這個句型中，表示詢問某人、某地或某事「是什麼樣子／感受如何」。
generatedExample: What is it like to work on night shifts?

explanationZhTw: `shabby` 形容東西破舊、狀況不好；這裡是在描述外觀很舊、磨損明顯的物件。
generatedExample: Her coat looks shabby, but it is still warm.

explanationZhTw: `straps` 是指「帶子、肩帶、束帶」；在這裡是說這個物件有兩條帶子可拿或可背。
generatedExample: The backpack has two strong straps.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「破舊很好」，而是表示東西雖然很舊、很寒酸，但仍可接受；帶有小說裡刻意的混合形容效果。
generatedExample: My backpack is shabby-good, but it still works for class.

explanationZhTw: 表示「不久的將來」，不是精確時間，而是大概很快會發生。
generatedExample: In the fairly near future, we may finish the project earlier than planned.

explanationZhTw: 指「印錯字的字」；misprinted 表示排版或印刷出現錯誤。
generatedExample: There was a misprinted word in the brochure, so we had to reprint it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是指請對方「再送一次、再給一份」內容，不是一般口語中的單純重複動作。
generatedExample: Could you ask for a repeat if the radio message is unclear?

explanationZhTw: 公事包、手提公文包；這裡指上班時帶的包。
generatedExample: He left his brief-case at home and had to go back for it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「裡面裝有、包含」，指那個公事包裡有一本書。
generatedExample: The box will contain all the tools you need.

explanationZhTw: 「silent」表示「沉默的、沒有說話的」，這裡用來描述他們短暫不出聲的狀態，屬於常見基礎字彙。
generatedExample: The room went silent after the announcement.

explanationZhTw: 「for a moment」表示「片刻、一下子」，強調時間很短；這裡只是說他們安靜了一小段時間。
generatedExample: She paused for a moment before answering.

explanationZhTw: 「a couple of」表示「幾個、兩三個」，這裡指大約還有幾分鐘，屬於日常常見表達。
generatedExample: I only need a couple of minutes to finish this.

explanationZhTw: 這句不是單純字面上的「沒有黑暗的地方」，而是帶有象徵或暗示意味的說法；要依上下文理解它指的是某種理想、神祕或超越現實的地方。
generatedExample: He spoke about a place where there is no darkness, and everyone felt uneasy.

explanationZhTw: 「hesitantly」表示「猶豫地、吞吞吐吐地」，形容說話或動作不太確定，帶著遲疑感。
generatedExample: She answered hesitantly because she was not sure what to say.

explanationZhTw: `nodded` 是 `nod` 的過去式，意思是「點頭」。這裡表示 O'Brien 只是用點頭回應，語氣很平靜。
generatedExample: She nodded to show she understood the plan.

explanationZhTw: `allusion` 指「暗指、影射」，不是直接說出來，而是用含蓄方式提到某件事。這裡表示他聽懂了那句話在暗示什麼。
generatedExample: I didn’t catch the allusion at first, but then I understood what she meant.

explanationZhTw: `in the meantime` 是慣用語，意思是「在此期間、與此同時」。用來表示在某件事發生前的空檔時間。
generatedExample: The repairs will take a week; in the meantime, you can use the spare room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `further` 不是「更遠」，而是「額外的、更多的」。`further question` 指「更多要問的問題」。
generatedExample: I have no further questions about the report.

explanationZhTw: `impulse` 指一股突然想做某事的衝動或念頭。
generatedExample: She had the impulse to call him right away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `utter` 是較正式的「說、講出」，不是一般的日常說法。
generatedExample: He was too nervous to utter a word.

explanationZhTw: `high-sounding` 指「聽起來很了不起、很漂亮」，但 `generalities` 表示「空泛的話」。合起來就是「聽起來很冠冕堂皇但內容空泛的話」。
generatedExample: The speech was full of high-sounding generalities but no real plan.

explanationZhTw: `composite` 表示「由多個部分組成的」。`composite picture` 在這裡是「由多個記憶片段拼成的整體畫面」。
generatedExample: The detective formed a composite picture of the suspect from several witness accounts.

explanationZhTw: `at random` 是固定用法，意思是「隨意地、沒有特定選擇地」。
generatedExample: He picked a book at random from the shelf.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是「發生」，而是表示「碰巧、順便、偶然」的意思。整個片語 `happen to` 用來把問題說得比較委婉，等於在問「你有沒有剛好聽過……？」
generatedExample: Did you happen to hear that song on the radio yesterday?

explanationZhTw: `rhyme` 是「押韻的短詩、童謠」；這裡的 `old rhyme` 指一首流傳已久的老童謠或兒歌。
generatedExample: She taught me an old rhyme about the moon and the stars.

explanationZhTw: 表示「點頭」。這是很常見的基本動作動詞，這裡指 O'Brien 用點頭回應。
generatedExample: She nodded to show that she understood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 grave 不是「墳墓」，而是「嚴肅的、莊重的」；grave courtesy 指帶著一種嚴肅、拘謹的禮貌。
generatedExample: He answered with grave courtesy, as if every word mattered.

explanationZhTw: 詩節，指詩中的一段，通常由幾行詩組成。
generatedExample: The poem begins with a short stanza about the sea.

explanationZhTw: `owe me` 是「欠我、虧欠我」的意思；在這裡表示對方還欠錢給說話者。
generatedExample: You still owe me ten dollars from lunch.

explanationZhTw: `three farthings` 指「三個法丁幣」；`farthing` 是英國舊制的極小面額硬幣，現在已不常用，所以屬於帶有歷史色彩的特殊用法。
generatedExample: My grandfather mentioned a time when a loaf of bread cost only a few farthings.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「last line」不是指真正的線條，而是指文字、詩句或演說中的「最後一行／最後一句」。
generatedExample: I forgot the ending, but I still remembered the last line of the poem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `I am afraid` 不是表示「我害怕」，而是禮貌地緩和語氣，等於「恐怕／很抱歉地說」。
generatedExample: I am afraid I won't be able to come tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: `had better` 是固定說法，表示「最好還是……；否則可能有不好的結果」。
generatedExample: You had better leave now if you want to catch the train.

explanationZhTw: `tablets` 在這裡是指「藥片；錠劑」，不是平板電腦。
generatedExample: Take two tablets after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「held out」這裡是「伸出、遞出」的意思。
generatedExample: She held out a hand to help him up.

explanationZhTw: 「grip」在這裡指「握力；緊握的力道」。
generatedExample: His grip was so strong that I could hardly shake his hand.

explanationZhTw: 「put someone out of mind」是慣用說法，意思是「不再想起某人；把某人拋到腦後」。
generatedExample: After the argument, she tried putting the problem out of mind and focusing on work.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「telescreen」是小說中的專有裝置，指兼具螢幕與監視功能的設備。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: 「deep-laden」在這裡表示「堆得很滿、裝載得很重」，帶有文學性的用法。
generatedExample: The shelves were deep-laden with dusty books.
